note: Archive/Sensitivity.lean:56,97 (namespace Sensitivity) - abbrev Q (n : ℕ) := Fin n → Bool with def adjacent {n} (p : Q n) : Set (Q n) := {q | ∃! i, p i ≠ q i} is exactly the hypercube graph (vertices = length-n bit strings, edges = differ in one coordinate), used ad hoc for Huang's sensitivity theorem rather than packaged as a general SimpleGraph instance. Verified full name Sensitivity.Q.adjacent against declaration-data.json oracle (fixed missing namespace prefix from an earlier draft).

Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/CharacteristicFunction/Basic.lean:246 - proves two finite measures on a complete second-countable inner-product space are equal if their (multivariate) characteristic functions agree everywhere; mathematically equivalent content to Cramér-Wold (a random vector's law is determined by all 1-D linear-projection laws, since charFun μ t is the 1-D charFun of the projection along t evaluated at 1) but stated via the joint char function directly, not phrased as 1-D projections.
